title: N002: Add Deployment, ServiceAccount, and RBAC templates
description:
**Size:** 🏗️ large
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** critical
**Source:** Proposed for 'Helm Chart Packaging

Chart structure under charts/'

Translate the core Kustomize manifests into parameterized Helm templates. Includes: templates/deployment.yaml (from config/manager/manager.yaml with parameterized image, replicas, resources, security context, container args including --watch-namespaces), templates/serviceaccount.yaml, and templates/rbac/ directory containing ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding, leader-election Role/RoleBinding, and metrics auth roles — all gated by rbac.create value. Each template uses helpers from _helpers.tpl for labels/selectors and respects existing app.kubernetes.io label conventions.

**Rationale:** These are the minimum resources required for a functional operator deployment. The Deployment, ServiceAccount, and RBAC resources form the operational core that must render correctly before optional components (webhooks, monitoring) can be layered on top.

**Affected Areas:**
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/deployment.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/serviceaccount.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/rbac/clusterrole.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/rbac/clusterrolebinding.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/rbac/leader-election-role.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/rbac/leader-election-rolebinding.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/rbac/metrics-role.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/rbac/metrics-rolebinding.yaml
- config/manager/manager.yaml
- config/rbac/

stories.title: Cluster admin deploys operator with auto-created ServiceAccount
stories.want: the Helm chart to create a dedicated ServiceAccount for the operator
stories.so_that: the operator pod runs with a proper identity and RBAC bindings reference it correctly
criteria: ServiceAccount rendered by default with name from fullname helper
Standard Helm labels (helm.sh/chart, app.kubernetes.io/name, instance, version, managed-by) applied to ServiceAccount metadata
Custom annotations from serviceAccount.annotations merged into ServiceAccount metadata.annotations
ServiceAccount not rendered when serviceAccount.create=false

stories.title: Cluster admin gets correct RBAC permissions for the operator
stories.want: the chart to create ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding with all operator permissions
stories.so_that: the operator can manage Memcached CRs, Deployments, Services, PDBs, ServiceMonitors, NetworkPolicies, and Secrets
criteria: ClusterRole contains all permission rules matching config/rbac/role.yaml (11 rule blocks for core, apps, autoscaling, memcached.c5c3.io, monitoring.coreos.com, networking.k8s.io, policy, events)
ClusterRoleBinding references the correct ServiceAccount name (from serviceAccountName helper) and release namespace
RBAC resources not rendered when rbac.create=false
All RBAC resources include standard Helm labels

stories.title: Cluster admin can run operator with leader election for HA
stories.want: leader-election RBAC (Role + RoleBinding) and --leader-elect arg to be managed by the chart
stories.so_that: multiple operator replicas can safely coordinate via lease-based leader election
criteria: Leader-election Role rendered in release namespace with configmaps, leases, and events permissions
Leader-election RoleBinding references the correct ServiceAccount and namespace
--leader-elect=true passed as container arg when leaderElection.enabled=true (default)
Leader-election Role/RoleBinding gated by rbac.create value

stories.title: Cluster admin configures operator Deployment parameters via Helm values
stories.want: to customize the operator image, replicas, resources, security contexts, and scheduling constraints
stories.so_that: the Deployment matches the cluster's requirements, policies, and resource quotas
criteria: image.repository and image.tag (defaulting to Chart.appVersion) set the container image
replicaCount controls spec.replicas
resources.limits and resources.requests applied to the manager container
podSecurityContext applied to pod spec, securityContext applied to container spec
nodeSelector, tolerations, affinity applied to pod spec when non-empty
imagePullSecrets rendered when non-empty

stories.title: Cluster admin restricts operator scope to specific namespaces
stories.want: to pass --watch-namespaces to the operator via Helm values
stories.so_that: the operator only reconciles Memcached resources in designated namespaces
criteria: No --watch-namespaces arg rendered when watchNamespaces is empty (default)
--watch-namespaces=ns1,ns2 arg rendered when watchNamespaces is [ns1, ns2]
Single namespace value renders correctly as --watch-namespaces=ns1

stories.title: Cluster admin uses a pre-existing ServiceAccount instead of chart-created one
stories.want: to reference an externally managed ServiceAccount by name
stories.so_that: ServiceAccount lifecycle can be managed by external tooling (Terraform, GitOps)
criteria: serviceAccount.create=false prevents ServiceAccount template from rendering
serviceAccount.name overrides the SA reference in Deployment and all RoleBindings
When create=false and name is empty, 'default' SA is used
RBAC bindings still reference the correct SA name regardless of create setting

stories.title: Cluster admin sees consistent labels on all chart-managed resources
stories.want: all resources created by the chart to have standard Helm labels
stories.so_that: resources can be identified, filtered, and managed by standard Kubernetes label selectors
criteria: Every template includes helm.sh/chart, app.kubernetes.io/name, app.kubernetes.io/instance, app.kubernetes.io/version, app.kubernetes.io/managed-by labels
Deployment selector uses selectorLabels (app.kubernetes.io/name + instance)
nameOverride and fullnameOverride propagate to all resource names and labels
control-plane: controller-manager label on Deployment and pod template for backward compatibility with existing selectors

tasks.title: Create ServiceAccount template (templates/serviceaccount.yaml) gated by serviceAccount.create, with labels, annotations, and custom name support — plus helm-unittest test file (tests/serviceaccount_test.yaml) covering create toggle, custom name, custom annotations, and label propagation (REQ-001, REQ-009)

Create ClusterRole template (templates/rbac/clusterrole.yaml) gated by rbac.create, containing all permission rules from config/rbac/role.yaml (11 rule blocks for secrets, services, events, deployments, HPAs, memcacheds, memcacheds/finalizers, memcacheds/status, servicemonitors, networkpolicies, PDBs) — plus ClusterRoleBinding template (templates/rbac/clusterrolebinding.yaml) referencing the SA name and release namespace. Add helm-unittest test file (tests/rbac_clusterrole_test.yaml) covering: rbac.create toggle, all permission rules present, correct roleRef and subjects, label propagation (REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-009)

Create leader-election Role template (templates/rbac/leader-election-role.yaml) gated by rbac.create, containing configmaps, leases, and events permissions from config/rbac/leader_election_role.yaml — plus RoleBinding template (templates/rbac/leader-election-rolebinding.yaml) referencing SA name and release namespace. Add helm-unittest test file (tests/rbac_leader_election_test.yaml) covering: rbac.create toggle, correct permissions, Role is namespace-scoped, RoleBinding subjects reference correct SA (REQ-002, REQ-004, REQ-009, REQ-010)

Create metrics auth ClusterRole template (templates/rbac/metrics-role.yaml) gated by rbac.create, containing tokenreviews and subjectaccessreviews permissions from config/rbac/metrics_auth_role.yaml — plus ClusterRoleBinding template (templates/rbac/metrics-rolebinding.yaml) referencing SA name and release namespace. Add helm-unittest test file (tests/rbac_metrics_test.yaml) covering: rbac.create toggle, correct permissions, binding references correct SA (REQ-002, REQ-004, REQ-009, REQ-011)

Create Deployment template (templates/deployment.yaml) translating config/manager/manager.yaml merged with config/default/manager_metrics_patch.yaml — parameterize: image (repository:tag with appVersion default), replicaCount, resources, podSecurityContext, securityContext, container args (--leader-elect conditional, --health-probe-bind-address=:8081, --metrics-bind-address=:8443, --metrics-secure, --watch-namespaces conditional), livenessProbe, readinessProbe, ports, serviceAccountName from helper, imagePullSecrets, nodeSelector, tolerations, affinity. Include control-plane: controller-manager label on pod template. Use selectorLabels for spec.selector.matchLabels and full labels for metadata (REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-007, REQ-008, REQ-009)

Create Deployment test file (tests/deployment_test.yaml) — suite 1: default rendering tests covering apiVersion, kind, replicas=1, default image=controller:0.1.0, default pullPolicy, default resources matching values.yaml, serviceAccountName from fullname helper, standard labels on metadata and pod template, selectorLabels on spec.selector.matchLabels (REQ-005, REQ-009)

Extend Deployment test file (tests/deployment_test.yaml) — suite 2: container args tests covering --leader-elect present by default, --leader-elect absent when leaderElection.enabled=false, no --watch-namespaces by default, --watch-namespaces=ns1,ns2 when set, --health-probe-bind-address and --metrics-bind-address always present. Suite 3: custom image tests covering custom tag, custom repository+tag, imagePullSecrets rendering (REQ-005, REQ-007, REQ-008)

Extend Deployment test file (tests/deployment_test.yaml) — suite 4: security context tests covering default podSecurityContext (runAsNonRoot, runAsUser=65532, seccompProfile), default container securityContext (allowPrivilegeEscalation=false, readOnlyRootFilesystem, capabilities.drop). Suite 5: scheduling tests covering nodeSelector, tolerations, affinity when set, and not present when empty (REQ-006)

Create cross-template integration test file (tests/integration_test.yaml) — test full chart render with all defaults produces correct resource count, test rbac.create=false suppresses all 6 RBAC resources while Deployment+SA still render, test serviceAccount.create=false suppresses SA while Deployment still references correct SA name, test fullnameOverride propagates to all resource names, test custom serviceAccount.name propagated to Deployment and all bindings (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-004)

Update NOTES.txt (charts/memcached-operator/templates/NOTES.txt) to include post-install diagnostics for RBAC and ServiceAccount — show ServiceAccount name, show whether RBAC was created or needs manual setup, conditionally display warning when rbac.create=false. Add test cases in tests/chart_metadata_test.yaml for the new NOTES.txt output (REQ-001, REQ-002)

Run helm lint and helm-unittest against the chart to verify all templates render without errors — fix any rendering issues, whitespace problems, or YAML syntax errors discovered. Verify `helm template` produces valid Kubernetes manifests for default values and for rbac.create=false + serviceAccount.create=false (REQ-001 through REQ-011)

Write Reference documentation (docs/reference/backend/helm-core-templates.md) covering: Deployment template parameters (image, replicas, resources, security contexts, args, scheduling), ServiceAccount template parameters (create, name, annotations), RBAC templates (rbac.create, list of created roles/bindings and their permissions), values.yaml reference table for all core-template-related values with types, defaults, and descriptions (REQ-001 through REQ-011)

test_specifications.test_function: should render ServiceAccount by default
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin deploys operator with auto-created ServiceAccount
test_specifications.expected: ServiceAccount rendered with name from fullname helper and standard Helm labels

test_specifications.test_function: should not render when serviceAccount.create is false
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin uses a pre-existing ServiceAccount instead of chart-created one
test_specifications.expected: Template produces 0 documents when serviceAccount.create=false

test_specifications.test_function: should use custom name from serviceAccount.name
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin uses a pre-existing ServiceAccount instead of chart-created one
test_specifications.expected: ServiceAccount metadata.name equals the custom serviceAccount.name value

test_specifications.test_function: should include custom annotations
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin deploys operator with auto-created ServiceAccount
test_specifications.expected: Custom annotations from serviceAccount.annotations appear in metadata.annotations

test_specifications.test_function: should render ClusterRole with all permission rules by default
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin gets correct RBAC permissions for the operator
test_specifications.expected: ClusterRole contains all 11 rule blocks matching config/rbac/role.yaml

test_specifications.test_function: should not render when rbac.create is false
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin gets correct RBAC permissions for the operator
test_specifications.expected: Both ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding templates produce 0 documents

test_specifications.test_function: should reference correct ServiceAccount in ClusterRoleBinding
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin gets correct RBAC permissions for the operator
test_specifications.expected: ClusterRoleBinding subjects[0] has correct name and namespace

test_specifications.test_function: should include standard Helm labels on ClusterRole
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin sees consistent labels on all chart-managed resources
test_specifications.expected: ClusterRole metadata.labels includes all standard Helm labels

test_specifications.test_function: should render Role with configmaps, leases, and events permissions
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin can run operator with leader election for HA
test_specifications.expected: Role rules include configmaps, leases (coordination.k8s.io), and events with correct verbs

test_specifications.test_function: should not render when rbac.create is false
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin can run operator with leader election for HA
test_specifications.expected: Leader-election Role and RoleBinding produce 0 documents

test_specifications.test_function: should reference correct ServiceAccount in RoleBinding
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin can run operator with leader election for HA
test_specifications.expected: RoleBinding subjects[0] references SA name from helper and release namespace

test_specifications.test_function: should render metrics auth ClusterRole with tokenreviews and subjectaccessreviews
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin gets correct RBAC permissions for the operator
test_specifications.expected: ClusterRole rules include authentication.k8s.io/tokenreviews and authorization.k8s.io/subjectaccessreviews with create verb

test_specifications.test_function: should not render when rbac.create is false
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin gets correct RBAC permissions for the operator
test_specifications.expected: Metrics auth ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding produce 0 documents

test_specifications.test_function: should render Deployment with default image controller:appVersion
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin configures operator Deployment parameters via Helm values
test_specifications.expected: Container image is controller:0.1.0 (repository:appVersion) with pullPolicy IfNotPresent

test_specifications.test_function: should use custom image tag when set
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin configures operator Deployment parameters via Helm values
test_specifications.expected: Container image uses the custom tag instead of appVersion

test_specifications.test_function: should render default security contexts matching Kustomize source
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin configures operator Deployment parameters via Helm values
test_specifications.expected: Pod securityContext has runAsNonRoot=true, runAsUser=65532; container has allowPrivilegeEscalation=false, readOnlyRootFilesystem=true

test_specifications.test_function: should include --leader-elect arg by default
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin can run operator with leader election for HA
test_specifications.expected: Container args include '--leader-elect'

test_specifications.test_function: should not include --leader-elect when disabled
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin can run operator with leader election for HA
test_specifications.expected: Container args do not include '--leader-elect' when leaderElection.enabled=false

test_specifications.test_function: should not include --watch-namespaces by default
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin restricts operator scope to specific namespaces
test_specifications.expected: Container args do not contain --watch-namespaces when watchNamespaces is empty

test_specifications.test_function: should include --watch-namespaces with comma-joined values
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin restricts operator scope to specific namespaces
test_specifications.expected: Container args include '--watch-namespaces=ns1,ns2' when watchNamespaces=[ns1, ns2]

test_specifications.test_function: should apply nodeSelector when set
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin configures operator Deployment parameters via Helm values
test_specifications.expected: Pod spec includes nodeSelector from values

test_specifications.test_function: should render imagePullSecrets when configured
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin configures operator Deployment parameters via Helm values
test_specifications.expected: Pod spec includes imagePullSecrets array from values

test_specifications.test_function: should suppress all RBAC resources when rbac.create is false
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin gets correct RBAC permissions for the operator
test_specifications.expected: All RBAC templates produce 0 documents while Deployment and SA still render

test_specifications.test_function: should use custom serviceAccount.name in Deployment and all bindings
test_specifications.story: Cluster admin uses a pre-existing ServiceAccount instead of chart-created one
test_specifications.expected: Deployment serviceAccountName and all binding subjects reference the custom SA name

review_criteria: All 8 template files listed in affected areas are created and render valid YAML via `helm template`
All RBAC permission rules in ClusterRole exactly match config/rbac/role.yaml — no missing or extra rules
rbac.create=false produces 0 documents for all 6 RBAC templates (ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBinding, leader-election Role, leader-election RoleBinding, metrics-role, metrics-rolebinding)
serviceAccount.create=false produces 0 documents for ServiceAccount but Deployment still references correct SA name

Default Deployment container args match merged config/manager/manager.yaml + config/default/manager_metrics_patch.yaml (--leader-elect, --health-probe-bind-address=:8081, --metrics-bind-address=:8443, --metrics-secure)
All templates use _helpers.tpl helpers (labels, selectorLabels, fullname, serviceAccountName) — no hardcoded names or labels
helm-unittest test suites pass for all template files with comprehensive coverage of default values, toggle values, and override values
helm lint passes with no warnings or errors on the complete chart
implementation_notes: The Deployment template translates config/manager/manager.yaml merged with config/default/manager_metrics_patch.yaml to produce the complete operator Deployment as deployed by the default Kustomize overlay. Container args include: --leader-elect (gated by leaderElection.enabled), --health-probe-bind-address=:8081, --metrics-bind-address=:8443, --metrics-secure, and optionally --watch-namespaces=<comma-separated> when watchNamespaces is non-empty. The image tag defaults to Chart.appVersion when image.tag is empty, using the pattern: {{ .Values.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.image.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}. All RBAC resources use {{ include "memcached-operator.fullname" . }}-<suffix> naming convention (e.g., fullname-manager-role, fullname-leader-election-role, fullname-metrics-auth-role) to avoid name collisions across releases. Role bindings reference {{ include "memcached-operator.serviceAccountName" . }} and {{ .Release.Namespace }}. Leader-election Role is namespace-scoped (kind: Role, not ClusterRole) and created in the release namespace. Follow the existing crd-template.yaml pattern for conditional rendering: {{- if .Values.rbac.create }} ... {{- end }}. The Deployment pod template includes the control-plane: controller-manager label for backward compatibility with existing monitoring or log selectors. Note: config/rbac/metrics_reader_role.yaml (ClusterRole: metrics-reader for /metrics nonResourceURL) is NOT in scope for this feature — it should be added when the metrics/monitoring templates feature is implemented.
